samples.gvd:
module signal_generator
(
    input clk,
    input rst_n,
    output reg [4:0] wave
);

    reg [1:0] state;

    always @(posedge clk or negedge rst_n) begin
        if (~rst_n) begin
            state <= 2'b00;
            wave <= 5'd0;
        end
        else begin
            case (state)
                2'b00: begin
                    if (wave == 5'd31) begin
                        state <= 2'b01;
                    end
                    else begin
                        wave <= wave + 1;
                    end
                end
                2'b01: begin
                    if (wave == 5'd0) begin
                        state <= 2'b00;
                    end
                    else begin
                        wave <= wave - 1;
                    end
                end
            endcase
        end
    end

endmodule
samples.xml_description:
```xml
<module id="signal_generator">
    <description>Triangle Wave Signal Generator</description>
    <ports>
        <input id="clk">Clock signal for synchronization.</input>
        <input id="rst_n">Active-low reset signal.</input>
        <output id="wave" width_description="[4:0]">5-bit output waveform signal.</output>
    </ports>
    <parameter_description>
        <state name="STATE_IDLE" value="0">Idle state.</state>
        <state name="STATE_INCREASING" value="1">Increasing state.</state>
        <state name="STATE_DECREASING" value="2">Decreasing state.</state>
    </parameter_description>
    <logic_description type="sequential_logic">
        <register id="state" width="2" initial_value="0">State register controlling waveform generation.</register>
        <register id="wave" width="5" initial_value="0">Waveform register representing the generated waveform.</register>
    </logic_description>
    <implementation>
        Always block triggered on posedge clk or negedge rst_n.
        On reset, clear state and wave registers.
        State machine controls waveform generation:
        - STATE_IDLE: Increment wave until it reaches 31, then transition to STATE_INCREASING.
        - STATE_INCREASING: Decrement wave until it reaches 0, then transition to STATE_DECREASING.
        - STATE_DECREASING: Increment wave until it reaches 31, then return to STATE_IDLE.
    </implementation>
</module>
```
